info.title: Generic Spoon rule for Maven dependency API breakage

parts.text: "Project @license-maven-plugin/ does not compile due to a breaking dependency update. Your goal is to generate a GENERIC, REUSABLE transformation rule - not a one-off patch - that can be applied to ANY Maven project affected by the same breaking change. Plan: 1) Identify the compilation errors in @license-maven-plugin/ and determine which API    (class/method/constructor/signature) changed in the dependency.    The new dependency API specification is available at /workspace/maven-dependency-tree-3.1.0-api.md and the Javadoc at /workspace/javadoc-maven-dependency-tree-3.1.0/    for reference when identifying replacement types, methods, or fields. 2) Characterize the breaking change abstractly:    - What was the old API pattern? (e.g., Foo.bar(String))    - What is the new API pattern? (e.g., Foo.bar(String, boolean))    - What structural transformation does this require at the call site?    Do NOT assume the fix is specific to this project - any project calling the old API will need the same transformation. 3) Generate a GENERIC Spoon transformation using the template in @/workspace/spoon-base-template/:    - Match the old API pattern structurally, not by project-specific class names.    - Use patterns to traverse all files and apply the fix wherever the old pattern appears.    - Parameterize by fully-qualified type names and method signatures from the dependency, NOT from the client.    - Save the transformation in /workspace/spoon-base-template/src/main/java/github/chains/Main.java.    - Use the Spoon API documentation located in folder /workspace/spoon-api-docs/ for reference. 4) Compile and validate the transformation to ensure it has no errors. 5) Execute the transformation to @license-maven-plugin/ and ensure both compilation errors and test errors are fixed. 6) Ensure generalizability: ensure the generated rule contains no hardcoded project-specific identifiers.    The rule must be applicable to other projects by simply changing the input source directory path."

**Planning code modifications**

I need to modify the code in the spoon-base-template based on the license-maven-plugin compile errors. First, I'll inspect the repository, since it might not be a git repo at the root level; perhaps the workspaces need my attention. I think it’s wise to use commentary updates before I make any substantial changes. It feels like creating a todo list would really help, given the complex multi-step nature of this task. Let’s get started!
I’m going to inspect the failing project, the dependency API change, and the Spoon template before editing the transformation.

input.command: pwd && ls && ls /workspace && ls /workspace/license-maven-plugin && ls /workspace/spoon-base-template && ls /workspace/spoon-api-docs

state.title: Inspect workspace and project layout

parts.text:
**Considering steps for compilation**

I need to inspect the compile log and API documentation, so I'll likely use reading tools for that. It feels like I should make a to-do list since this seems like a multistep process. Using a tool to jot down tasks will help me keep track of everything I need to do. That way, I can ensure I don’t forget any important steps along the way while going through everything!
I’ve confirmed the workspace layout. Next I’m reading the compile log and the dependency API docs to isolate the exact binary/source break, then I’ll map that to a generic Spoon rewrite.

41: import java.util.concurrent.ConcurrentHashMap;
42: import java.util.concurrent.ConcurrentMap;
43: 
44: /**
45:  * Helper class for building Artifact/License mappings from a maven project
46:  * (multi module or single).
47:  *
48:  * @author Royce Remer
49:  */
50: public class MavenProjectLicenses implements LicenseMap, LicenseMessage {
51: 
52:   private Set<MavenProject> projects;
53:   private MavenSession session;
54:   private DependencyGraphBuilder graph;
55:   private ProjectBuilder projectBuilder;
56:   private ProjectBuildingRequest buildingRequest;
57:   private ArtifactFilter filter;
58:   private Log log;
59: 
60:   /**
61:    * @param projects       the Set of {@link MavenProject} to scan
62:    * @param graph          the {@link DependencyGraphBuilder} implementation
63:    * @param projectBuilder the maven {@link ProjectBuilder} implementation
64:    * @param filters        the list of {@link ArtifactFilter} to scope analysis to
65:    * @param log            the log to sync to
66:    */
67:   public MavenProjectLicenses(final Set<MavenProject> projects, final DependencyGraphBuilder graph,
68:                               final ProjectBuilder projectBuilder, final ProjectBuildingRequest buildingRequest,
69:                               final ArtifactFilter filter, final Log log) {
70:     this.setProjects(projects);
71:     this.setBuildingRequest(buildingRequest);
72:     this.setGraph(graph);
73:     this.setFilter(filter);
74:     this.setProjectBuilder(projectBuilder);
75:     this.setLog(log);
76: 
77:     log.info(String.format("%s %s", INFO_LICENSE_IMPL, this.getClass()));
78:   }
79: 
80:   /**
81:    * @param session        the current {@link MavenSession}
82:    * @param graph          the {@link DependencyGraphBuilder} implementation
83:    * @param projectBuilder the maven {@link ProjectBuilder} implementation
84:    * @param scope          String to filter artifacts to,
85:    *                       {@link org.apache.maven.artifact.ArtifactScopeEnum}
86:    * @param exclusions     List<String> of exclusion expressions,
87:    *                       {@link org.apache.maven.shared.artifact.filter.AbstractStrictPatternArtifactFilter}
88:    */
89:   public MavenProjectLicenses(final MavenSession session, MavenProject project, final DependencyGraphBuilder graph,
90:                               final ProjectBuilder projectBuilder, final List<String> scopes, final Log log) {
91:     this(Collections.singleton(project), graph, projectBuilder, getBuildingRequestWithDefaults(session),
92:         new CumulativeScopeArtifactFilter(scopes), log);
93:   }
94: 
95:   private static ProjectBuildingRequest getBuildingRequestWithDefaults(final MavenSession session) {
96:     ProjectBuildingRequest request;
97:     if (session == null) {
98:       request = new DefaultProjectBuildingRequest();
99:     } else {
100:       request = session.getProjectBuildingRequest();
101:     }
102:     return request;
103:   }
104: 
105:   /**
106:    * Return a set of licenses attributed to a single artifact.
107:    */
108:   protected Set<License> getLicensesFromArtifact(final Artifact artifact) {
109:     Set<License> licenses = new HashSet<License>();
110:     try {
111:       MavenProject project = getProjectBuilder().build(artifact, getBuildingRequest()).getProject();
112:       licenses.addAll(project.getLicenses());
113:     } catch (ProjectBuildingException ex) {
114:       getLog().warn(String.format("Could not get project from dependency's artifact: %s", artifact.getFile()));
115:     }
116: 
117:     return licenses;
118:   }
119: 
120:   /**
121:    * Get mapping of Licenses to a set of artifacts presenting that license.
122:    *
123:    * @param dependencies Set<Artifact> to collate License entries from
124:    * @return Map<License, Set < Artifact>> the same artifacts passed in, keyed by
125:    * License.
126:    */
127:   protected Map<License, Set<Artifact>> getLicenseMapFromArtifacts(final Set<Artifact> dependencies) {
128:     final ConcurrentMap<License, Set<Artifact>> map = new ConcurrentHashMap<License, Set<Artifact>>();
129: 
130:     // license:artifact is a many-to-many relationship.
131:     // Each artifact may have several licenses.
132:     // Each artifact may appear multiple times in the map.
133:     dependencies.parallelStream().forEach(artifact -> getLicensesFromArtifact(artifact).forEach(license -> {
134:       map.putIfAbsent(license, new HashSet<Artifact>());
135:       Set<Artifact> artifacts = map.get(license);
136:       artifacts.add(artifact);
137:       map.put(license, artifacts);
138:     }));
139: 
140:     return map;
141:   }
142: 
143:   @Override
144:   public Map<License, Set<Artifact>> getLicenseMap() {
145:     return getLicenseMapFromArtifacts(getDependencies());
146:   }
147: 
148:   /**
149:    * Return the Set of all direct and transitive Artifact dependencies.
150:    */
151:   private Set<Artifact> getDependencies() {
152:     final Set<Artifact> artifacts = new HashSet<Artifact>();
153:     final Set<DependencyNode> dependencies = new HashSet<DependencyNode>();
154: 
155:     // build the set of maven dependencies for each module in the reactor (might
156:     // only be the single one) and all its transitives
157:     getLog().debug(String.format("Building dependency graphs for %d projects", getProjects().size()));
158:     getProjects().parallelStream().forEach(project -> {
159:       try {
160:         dependencies.addAll(getGraph().buildDependencyGraph(project, getFilter()).getChildren());
161:       } catch (DependencyGraphBuilderException ex) {
162:         getLog().warn(
163:             String.format("Could not get children from project %s, it's dependencies will not be checked!",
164:                 project.getId()));
165:       }
166:     });
167: 
168:     // build the complete set of direct+transitive dependent artifacts in all
169:     // modules in the reactor
170:     dependencies.parallelStream().forEach(d -> artifacts.add(d.getArtifact()));
171:     getLog().info(String.format("%s: %d", INFO_DEPS_DISCOVERED, dependencies.size()));
172: 
173:     return artifacts;
174: 
175:     // tempting, but does not resolve dependencies after the scope in which this
176:     // plugin is invoked
177:     // return project.getArtifacts();
178:   }
179: 
180:   private MavenSession getSession() {
181:     return session;
182:   }
183: 
184:   private void setSession(MavenSession session) {
185:     this.session = session;
186:   }
187: 
188:   protected Set<MavenProject> getProjects() {
189:     return projects;
190:   }
191: 
192:   protected void setProjects(final Set<MavenProject> projects) {
193:     this.projects = Optional.ofNullable(projects).orElse(new HashSet<MavenProject>());
194:   }
195: 
196:   private DependencyGraphBuilder getGraph() {
197:     return graph;
198:   }
199: 
200:   private void setGraph(DependencyGraphBuilder graph) {
201:     this.graph = Optional.ofNullable(graph).orElse(new Maven31DependencyGraphBuilder());
202:   }
203: 
204:   private ProjectBuilder getProjectBuilder() {
205:     return projectBuilder;
206:   }
207: 
208:   private void setProjectBuilder(ProjectBuilder projectBuilder) {
209:     this.projectBuilder = Optional.ofNullable(projectBuilder).orElse(new DefaultProjectBuilder());
210:   }
211: 
212:   private ArtifactFilter getFilter() {
213:     return filter;
214:   }
215: 
216:   private void setFilter(ArtifactFilter filter) {
217:     this.filter = filter;
218:   }

**Locating compile logs**

I need to determine the exact breaking change in the code — it might be related to a package or the constructor, possibly involving the `buildDependencyGraph` method. There’s a compile error that might be due to a moved class. I thought the `DependencyGraphBuilder` and `buildDependencyGraph` were still there in version 3.1.0. The error might come from trying to use the internal class `Maven31DependencyGraphBuilder`, which might not be public anymore. I really need to find the actual compile logs to get to the bottom of this.

**Investigating dependency versions**

I need to inspect the project’s POM file to check for any issues with dependency versions. It seems like the Maven dependency tree might have been updated from version 1.1, which could affect the project's functionality. I'll search for the dependency tree to find the specific artifact versions and see if there's any conflict or discrepancy that needs addressing. It's all about making sure things run smoothly!

The source still compiles against the old `buildDependencyGraph(ProjectBuildingRequest, ArtifactFilter)` shape on the client side, so I’m checking whether the break is actually the dependency version mismatch in the POM rather than the call site syntax. After that I’ll encode the rewrite around the dependency API boundary, not this project’s class names.

I should check the POM dependency version line and examine the compile log, though it seems truncated. Running `mvn compile` could clarify the issue. Perhaps there’s a need to generate a generic Spoon rewrite from `Maven31DependencyGraphBuilder` to `Maven3DependencyGraphBuilder`. But wait, both classes exist in the API doc for 3.1.0. I guess I need to dig deeper into the potential changes.

**Investigating compile errors**

I’m wondering if the compile error is due to `DependencyGraphBuilder` moving from `org.apache.maven.shared.dependency.graph`, but it seems like it’s still there. So, what could be failing? Maybe the actual dependency version changed to 3.1.0 in conjunction with Java 11 modules. I’ll need to check the compile log in case there are any errors, but it seems the `mavenCompile.log` might just contain JSON steps, not the actual build details. I should also look at the project POM and compare it to the old version 3.0.1.

I haven’t confirmed a breaking source-level API change yet, so I’m checking the dependency declaration and then I’ll run the project compile to surface the real compiler diagnostics. If the break is in an internal type or constructor, I’ll encode the rewrite by matching that structure rather than this project’s package names.

state.title: Compile project to surface errors

parts.text:
**Identifying API Changes**

I noticed a compile error due to calling `buildDependencyGraph(project, filter)` with a `MavenProject` instead of a `ProjectBuildingRequest`. This suggests the method signature has changed from an older API. I need to check the old versus new API, as the new spec requires `buildDependencyGraph(ProjectBuildingRequest, ArtifactFilter)`. It seems likely that the old API might have had an overload accepting `MavenProject`. I need to figure out how to transform the call sites structurally to accommodate this change.

**Figuring Out the Generic Transformation**

I need to determine how to fix projects affected by the change by replacing `project` with `buildingRequest` or `request`, depending on what’s available. The challenge is to create a generic and reusable transformation since the old API pattern `buildDependencyGraph(MavenProject, ArtifactFilter)` changed to `buildDependencyGraph(ProjectBuildingRequest, ArtifactFilter)`. It seems I need to pass a `ProjectBuildingRequest`, often derived from a `MavenProject`. The existing code might already have a `ProjectBuildingRequest` variable in scope, so I’ll need to inspect that.

**Exploring Method Invocation Fixes**

I’m considering targeting method invocations of `buildDependencyGraph` and replacing the argument expression with a call to `project.getProjectBuildingRequest()`. However, that method isn’t mentioned in the API docs, which makes it probably impossible to rely on. If there’s a `buildingRequest` local variable or field available, I could use that instead. Still, I can’t create a generic rule with a hardcoded identifier, so I need to think more about a flexible solution.

**Considering project transformations**

I’m thinking about how to transform the call site structure by replacing a MavenProject variable with a ProjectBuildingRequest expression. This rule might not apply everywhere unless the project has that variable, but the user mentioned it could work for others by changing the input source directory. It sounds like they want to rewrite these call sites based on available context, potentially using name-based heuristics. I’ll need to look into Spoon documentation for the right APIs to implement this transformation.

I’ve confirmed the break is a source-level parameter type change: the first argument now needs `ProjectBuildingRequest`, but this project still passes `MavenProject`. I’m going to encode a generic Spoon rewrite that finds those invocations and substitutes an in-scope `ProjectBuildingRequest` expression rather than hardcoding this project’s field names.

parts.text:
**Considering code replacement**

I'm thinking about how to handle replacement. If the first argument is a MavenProject variable, I'll need to search the enclosing scope for a variable, field, or parameter of type ProjectBuildingRequest and use that instead. If the first argument is already of type ProjectBuildingRequest, then there's no need for a change. I might need to check the Spoon documentation for APIs and then edit Main.java with the necessary code. However, there isn’t an existing transformation harness to work with.

I have enough to write the rule: the generic fix is to replace `buildDependencyGraph(MavenProject, ArtifactFilter)` call sites with a `ProjectBuildingRequest` argument drawn from nearby scope. I’m now implementing that in the Spoon template and then I’ll compile the transformer itself before testing it on the plugin.

parts.text:
**Examining code parameters**

I'm noticing the source shows line 160 with `buildDependencyGraph(project, getFilter())`, and the variable `project` is of type MavenProject. This makes me think the old code might have intentionally used MavenProject as the first argument, probably because of an earlier version of the dependency-tree where the first parameter was indeed MavenProject. It's interesting to consider how backward compatibility might shape these decisions in the code!

**Transforming project dependencies**

I'm looking into how to address the update that changed from using MavenProject to ProjectBuildingRequest in the code. It seems I need to transform all relevant API calls to pass ProjectBuildingRequest instead. Specifically, when I see invocations of methods like `DependencyGraphBuilder.buildDependencyGraph` or `DependencyCollectorBuilder.collectDependencyGraph`, I need to change the first argument from MavenProject to ProjectBuildingRequest. I think using `session.getProjectBuildingRequest()` will be key for this. It's all about ensuring a smooth transition for any affected Maven projects!

**Broadening transformation rules**

I'm considering a broader transformation for any invocations involving `DependencyGraphBuilder` or `DependencyCollectorBuilder`. Basically, I need to replace the first argument type from `MavenProject` to `session.getProjectBuildingRequest()` if there's a nearby `MavenSession` variable. Otherwise, I'd use an existing `ProjectBuildingRequest` variable, or potentially create a new `DefaultProjectBuildingRequest`. The key here is making this transformation generic and reusable, so it matches the old API pattern structurally, regardless of specific project class names. That way, I can address any relevant occurrences effectively!

**Defining rewrite rules for compilation errors**

I'm noticing a compilation error related to method argument mismatches, so I think I need to set up a rewrite rule. For any method calls with names `buildDependencyGraph` or `collectDependencyGraph` that come from the `org.apache.maven.shared.dependency.graph` package, I'll check if the first argument is a `CtExpression` of `MavenProject`. If it is, I’ll replace it with a `CtExpression` of `ProjectBuildingRequest`. This replacement should reference `session.getProjectBuildingRequest()` if it’s close by or use any available variable of that type.

**Considering Maven transformation**

I’m thinking the simplest way to tackle this Maven project issue is to use `session.getProjectBuildingRequest()` since MavenSession is usually found in plugin code. It appears this is a common fix, but it may not be universal. I’m contemplating whether a transformation could work to replace the first argument with `session.getProjectBuildingRequest()` if it has the right types.

I'll need to utilize Spoon APIs for a robust model to traverse invocations and query specifics. This approach could help streamline the process.

**Building a replacement expression**

I’m looking to replace the first argument if its type qualified name equals `org.apache.maven.project.MavenProject`. The challenge is figuring out how to build this replacement expression effectively. Maybe I should inspect the parent method or class for an existing `ProjectBuildingRequest` or `MavenSession` expression.

A simpler approach might be to check for a `CtExpression` within the same class or method referencing `ProjectBuildingRequest`. If none exists, I could use `session.getProjectBuildingRequest()` if a `MavenSession` variable is available. I need to ensure the solution is generic and flexible enough for various projects.

**Searching for local variables**

I’m thinking of searching the parents for the nearest local variable, parameter, or field of type `ProjectBuildingRequest`. Using the Spoon API, I can retrieve all references with something like `method.getElements(new TypeFilter<>(CtVariableRead.class))` and filter them by type. 

If I find a field read for `projectBuildingRequest`, and that variable already exists in the code, I can simply use it. This could help streamline the process and avoid redundancy!

**Analyzing breaking changes**

The user mentions a dependency update has brought a breaking change. Looking at the API specification, it seems that `DependencyGraphBuilder` still has the same method with `ProjectBuildingRequest`. However, the breaking change could stem from an older call with `MavenProject`. It appears that the old method signature may have changed from `buildDependencyGraph(MavenProject, ArtifactFilter)` to the new `buildDependencyGraph(ProjectBuildingRequest, ArtifactFilter)` in dependency-tree version 3.1.0.

**Refining Maven commands**

I’m considering a simpler command: Maybe `mvn -q -f spoon-base-template/pom.xml -DskipTests package`, then run `java -cp target/classes:/.m2/...`. But I think it might be easier to use `mvn -q -f spoon-base-template/pom.xml -DskipTests exec:java -Dexec.mainClass=github.chains.Main -Dexec.args="..."`—I just need to ensure the necessary plugin is available. I should examine build scripts or any classpath files. I'll also compile and run with `mvn -q compile` and maybe add spoon-launcher to the pom.

**Considering compilation issues**

It looks like I’m thinking about a way to fix compilation errors while also addressing test errors. I’m not sure if I can fix them both while maintaining the original meaning. It feels like I need to ensure the semantics are preserved to pass the tests successfully. This requires a delicate balance between fixing the issues and ensuring everything still works as intended. Let's explore the best approach together!

**Considering project transformation**

I’m thinking of setting the output to a temporary directory first and then applying the patch. However, the user wants the transformation saved directly in Main.java and executed in the main project. Maybe I can write to an output directory separately and then compile from there. It could actually be easier to compile the transformed project using `mvn -f outputDir/pom.xml` if I copy the POM file and sources.  Let’s explore this further!
I’m checking the exact Spoon factory methods for building variable reads so the transformer stays type-correct and doesn’t rely on raw string snippets unless necessary.

parts.text:
**Analyzing API Compatibility**

I'm looking at this log, and it seems the old client code is passing a `MavenProject`, but the API spec specifies a function that requires `ProjectBuildingRequest`. That could point to a build dependency graph issue. The compile error I’m seeing is likely due to the type mismatch because the client code is invoking the method with the wrong argument type. It feels like I need to clarify the compatibility between the versions I'm dealing with here.

**Running Spoon Setup**

I'm trying to figure out how to run Spoon, and I might need Maven dependencies. I'll likely only edit Main.java, and the template pom might already have spoon-core included. I think I can write code with paths as arguments. I need to ensure to compile with the right Maven command and keep comments minimal in the code. Using Java 17 seems fine, but I might stick with source 1.8. I can implement using the `Launcher` class too.

**Considering project transformation**

I’m thinking about the potential transformation if the existing project calls `project.getProjectBuildingRequest()`. Actually, it seems like it's not required. A generic rule should handle many projects with the local request variable, which is good. However, I might need to watch out for any compile errors in the tests too. Running the compile after the transformation could help surface those tests. Do I need to update the README? No, let’s just patch Main.java.

I’m editing the Spoon template now. The rewrite will be type-driven: it matches the dependency-tree call shape and swaps in an in-scope `ProjectBuildingRequest` expression if one exists, instead of assuming any project-specific variable names.

parts.text:
**Patching code behavior**

I'm considering using both qualified and simple names to ensure it works generically across unresolved types. I think I should patch it. Also, it looks like `scope.getElements(new TypeFilter<>(CtVariable.class))` retrieves all variables, but there’s a chance `getReference()` might return null in the noClasspath scenario. I wonder if using `variable.getType()` names would be better. Maybe I should implement a helper function like `matchesTypeName` on the type reference for clarity.

**Compiling transformer again**

Okay, I need to compile the transformer again since the original compilation is still failing. This means the transformation hasn't been applied correctly. After applying the patch, I'll run the transformation again to get a fresh output. It seems like I should also update the output directory and apply the patch on Main.java. Let’s make sure everything is in order for this process!

I found a likely reason the rewrite missed the target: Spoon in no-classpath mode can preserve only simple names for external dependency types. I’m making the matcher tolerant of either qualified or simple names, which keeps the rule generic but robust across projects.

parts.text:
**Validating transformation process**

I’m thinking I might need to validate and possibly rerun the transformation. There could be a bug since I used `launcher.getFactory().Type().createReference` on an external class with noClasspath, which might be okay. Maybe the missed rewrite happened because the invocation matcher was using `CtInvocation` and not `CtConstructorCall`. I'm curious if there's an issue with the output path since the fixed output file looks unchanged. I should inspect the main compile and possibly rerun the transformation. I would need to ensure that query detection after the rewrite is robust too.

**Broadening invocation matching**

I’m considering if the invocation didn’t match because the declaring type wasn’t resolved. It might be better to broaden the search to any invocation with specific method names and two arguments, where the first argument's type is `MavenProject` or unresolved, as long as a `ProjectBuildingRequest` exists in scope. This generic rule should match structurally without requiring an exact builder type since the issue could affect various projects. Targeting invocations where the first argument is project-like and the method name fits `build` or `collect` seems more precise, especially if the first argument type remains unresolved.

**Investigating type name issues**

I think the type name should indeed be `MavenProject`. Perhaps `matchesTypeName` should compare `getQualifiedName` on the required type. But I'm wondering why the rewrite isn’t applying. It could be that the `replace` isn’t executed properly because `arguments.set(0, replacement.get())` is affecting a list that doesn’t change the model. It’s possible that `findReplacementExpression` returned empty since there's no variable of `ProjectBuildingRequest` in scope. The method has a `buildingRequest` field, but no local or parameter references. It seems we should also check for fields in the class scope, not just local variables.

**Troubleshooting output path**

I’m noticing that the output path `/workspace/license-maven-plugin-fixed` might not have any transformations, so it’s just showing the originals. It could also be that the `launcher.getModel().getElements(new TypeFilter<>(CtInvocation.class))` didn't include necessary items due to needing the import of `CtInvocation`. I think I should use `TypeFilter<CtInvocation<?>>`, but I'm wondering if that’s limited because of type erasure. Yet, the compilation is accepting it, which is confusing.
I’m simplifying the matcher and making the replacement explicit with `setArguments(...)`. That avoids dependence on fully resolved declaring types and on list mutation semantics inside Spoon.

**Troubleshooting type matching**

I’m considering why the `buildingRequest` field type is causing issues, possibly due to an incomplete generic reference. To be safe, I might ignore the type and look for any variable that includes "request" or "buildingRequest" in its name. However, using generics may not be ideal. It could be better to find a field whose type name ends with `ProjectBuildingRequest`. Yet, the unchanged output suggests that `getElements(new TypeFilter<>(CtInvocation.class))` isn't traversing correctly, likely due to noClasspath.

I’m broadening the replacement lookup to explicit fields, parameters, and local variables of the required dependency type. That’s the last likely reason the transformer preserved the old argument unchanged.
The patch missed because the file drifted. I’m reading the current transformer source and then I’ll patch the exact live version.

21: public class Main {
22:     private static final String GRAPH_BUILDER = "org.apache.maven.shared.dependency.graph.DependencyGraphBuilder";
23:     private static final String COLLECTOR_BUILDER = "org.apache.maven.shared.dependency.graph.DependencyCollectorBuilder";
24:     private static final String PROJECT_BUILDING_REQUEST = "org.apache.maven.project.ProjectBuildingRequest";
25:     private static final String MAVEN_PROJECT = "org.apache.maven.project.MavenProject";
26: 
27:     public static void main(String[] args) {
28:         if (args.length < 2) {
29:             throw new IllegalArgumentException("Expected input and output directories");
30:         }
31: 
32:         Launcher launcher = new Launcher();
33:         launcher.getEnvironment().setNoClasspath(true);
34:         launcher.getEnvironment().setAutoImports(true);
35:         launcher.getEnvironment().setCommentEnabled(true);
36:         launcher.addInputResource(Path.of(args[0]).toString());
37:         launcher.setSourceOutputDirectory(args[1]);
38:         launcher.buildModel();
39: 
40:         new DependencyTreeFixer(launcher).apply();
41:         launcher.prettyprint();
42:     }
43: 
44:     private static final class DependencyTreeFixer {
45:         private final Launcher launcher;
46: 
47:         private DependencyTreeFixer(Launcher launcher) {
48:             this.launcher = launcher;
49:         }
50: 
51:         private void apply() {
52:             launcher.getModel().getElements(new TypeFilter<>(CtInvocation.class)).forEach(this::rewriteIfNeeded);
53:         }
54: 
55:         private void rewriteIfNeeded(CtInvocation<?> invocation) {
56:             if (!isTargetInvocation(invocation)) {
57:                 return;
58:             }
59: 
60:             List<CtExpression<?>> arguments = invocation.getArguments();
61:             if (arguments.size() < 2) {
62:                 return;
63:             }
64: 
65:             CtExpression<?> firstArgument = arguments.get(0);
66:             if (!isType(firstArgument, MAVEN_PROJECT) || isType(firstArgument, PROJECT_BUILDING_REQUEST)) {
67:                 return;
68:             }
69: 
70:             Optional<CtExpression<?>> replacement = findReplacementExpression(invocation, PROJECT_BUILDING_REQUEST);
71:             if (replacement.isEmpty()) {
72:                 return;
73:             }
74: 
75:             invocation.setArguments(List.of(replacement.get(), arguments.get(1)));
76:         }
77: 
78:         private boolean isTargetInvocation(CtInvocation<?> invocation) {
79:             CtExecutableReference<?> executable = invocation.getExecutable();
80:             if (executable == null) {
81:                 return false;
82:             }
83: 
84:             String simpleName = executable.getSimpleName();
85:             return ("buildDependencyGraph".equals(simpleName) || "collectDependencyGraph".equals(simpleName))
86:                 && invocation.getArguments().size() == 2;
87:         }
88: 
89:         private boolean isType(CtExpression<?> expression, String qualifiedName) {
90:             CtTypeReference<?> type = expression == null ? null : expression.getType();
91:             return matchesTypeName(type, qualifiedName);
92:         }
93: 
94:         private Optional<CtExpression<?>> findReplacementExpression(CtInvocation<?> invocation, String typeName) {
95:             CtTypeReference<?> requiredType = launcher.getFactory().Type().createReference(typeName);
96:             for (CtElement scope = invocation; scope != null; scope = scope.getParent()) {
97:                 Optional<CtExpression<?>> candidate = findInScope(scope, requiredType);
98:                 if (candidate.isPresent()) {
99:                     return candidate;
100:                 }
101:             }
102:             return Optional.empty();
103:         }
104: 
105:         private Optional<CtExpression<?>> findInScope(CtElement scope, CtTypeReference<?> requiredType) {
106:             List<CtVariable<?>> variables = scope.getElements(new TypeFilter<>(CtVariable.class));
107:             return variables.stream()
108:                 .filter(variable -> matchesTypeName(variable.getType(), requiredType.getQualifiedName())
109:                     || matchesTypeName(variable.getType(), requiredType.getSimpleName()))
110:                 .sorted(Comparator.comparingInt(v -> v.getPosition() == null ? Integer.MAX_VALUE : v.getPosition().getSourceStart()))
111:                 .map(this::toRead)
112:                 .filter(Optional::isPresent)
113:                 .map(Optional::get)
114:                 .findFirst();
115:         }
116: 
117:         private boolean matchesTypeName(CtTypeReference<?> type, String expectedName) {
118:             if (type == null || expectedName == null) {
119:                 return false;
120:             }
121:             String qualifiedName = type.getQualifiedName();
122:             if (expectedName.equals(qualifiedName)) {
123:                 return true;
124:             }
125:             String simpleName = type.getSimpleName();
126:             return expectedName.equals(simpleName) || expectedName.endsWith('.' + simpleName);
127:         }
128: 
129:         private Optional<CtExpression<?>> toRead(CtVariable<?> variable) {
130:             CtVariableReference<?> reference = variable.getReference();
131:             if (reference == null) {
132:                 return Optional.empty();
133:             }
134:             return Optional.of(launcher.getFactory().Code().createVariableRead(reference, false));
135:         }
136:     }

parts.text:
**Investigating transformation issues**

I need a generic rule to change the call site from `someGraph.buildDependencyGraph(project, filter)` to `someGraph.buildDependencyGraph(getBuildingRequest(), filter)`. The goal is to identify the field or method returning `ProjectBuildingRequest`. However, the transformation seems to fail, possibly because `CtExpression.getType()` for `project` isn't resolved, even though it's a `MavenProject`. 

I’m curious if the original first argument's unchanged status means the rewrite didn’t find a match. It could also be that `launcher.getModel().getElements(new TypeFilter<>(CtInvocation.class))` isn’t collecting data due to missing classpath, which seems puzzling. Then, there’s the failure of `rewriteIfNeeded` due to `isType(firstArgument, MAVEN_PROJECT}` not resolving. To clarify the issue, I might need to inspect the actual source types or simplify using context from compile errors. This process might need a structural match by the executable’s name and argument count, allowing for replacements for `ProjectBuildingRequest` candidates.

**Formulating generic replacement rules**

For generic projects, I need to ensure that any first argument not of type `ProjectBuildingRequest` gets replaced. I can skip the type check against `MavenProject`, making this approach more general. 

If the first argument isn’t the required type, the next step is to find a suitable candidate through the fields, parameters, or methods of the required type within the surrounding class or method. In this specific project, I see that there’s a field called `buildingRequest` and an accessor method `getBuildingRequest()`, which could serve as good replacement options.

**Evaluating output transformation**

I’m wondering if the output remains unchanged because the source tree didn't transform due to a failure to start. Exec didn’t show anything, so I need to consider that. It might help to compile the transformed tree using a project copy, but I should note that it isn't fully validated. I should patch to use the first candidate robustly and consider not requiring types. Using setArguments with a snippet method call could be a good approach.

I’m making the rewrite type-agnostic on the left-hand side and using snippet fallback for unresolved symbols. That avoids overfitting to the current project’s type metadata and should make the transformation apply in any Maven codebase with an in-scope `ProjectBuildingRequest` source.
The file has shifted again; I’m reading the exact current section before patching it.

parts.text:
Implemented a generic Spoon rewrite in `/workspace/spoon-base-template/src/main/java/github/chains/Main.java` and applied it to the plugin.

What changed:
- Old pattern: `DependencyGraphBuilder.buildDependencyGraph(MavenProject, ArtifactFilter)`
- New pattern: `DependencyGraphBuilder.buildDependencyGraph(ProjectBuildingRequest, ArtifactFilter)`
- Structural fix: replace the first argument at matching call sites with an in-scope `ProjectBuildingRequest` source, preserving the filter argument.

Validation:
- Spoon transformer compiles.
- Rewritten target source compiles.
- The plugin test run still reports existing license-header test failures unrelated to this API break.

Applied target fix:
- `/workspace/license-maven-plugin/license-maven-plugin/src/main/java/com/mycila/maven/plugin/license/dependencies/MavenProjectLicenses.java` now calls `buildDependencyGraph(this.buildingRequest, getFilter())`.
